what_it_does: This file is a design-breakdown noticeboard item that specifies an overlay / radial command menu (the 'V' symbol) for a mobile review/annotation interface, organized into SPECIFIED, IMPLIED, and ADDITIONS sections.
observations: YAML frontmatter marks it as a current block authored by claude_code in the dragnet-development channel
It links to item-11da929d as its parent/part
The menu is described as a radial overlay opened by a branded 'V' entry point
It lists controls: element-mode / block-mode toggle, submission controls, comment count, supervisor chat, replies/correspondences needing attention
It implies a persistent floating thumb-reachable button, badges for pending/unread, and one-handed iOS ergonomics
Additions include quick-submit, jump-to-next-reply, session indicator, settings, a 'needs your eyes' priority lane, gentle animation, and a thumb-oriented open direction
standouts: Thumb-first, one-handed iOS ergonomics with the menu opening toward the thumb
A dedicated 'needs your eyes' priority lane for supervisor decisions/responses
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history
Uses a SPECIFIED / IMPLIED / ADDITIONS breakdown structure
Is a prose design spec rather than source code; no code symbols
concerns: The ADDITIONS section accumulates many capabilities without prioritization, sizing, or feasibility constraints
No mention of accessibility, explicit close gesture, or how an open radial menu avoids blocking content interaction
notes: A mobile UI breakdown proposing a thumb-reachable 'V' radial command center with mode toggles, draft queue, supervisor chat, and a priority inbox lane.
questions: What exactly is the existing 'V' symbol and where does it appear in the product?
What do 'element-mode' and 'block-mode' mean in this context?
Is this iOS-only or also intended for Android/desktop?
How does the open menu avoid obscuring the content being reviewed?
Who is the 'supervisor' and how is their chat/inbox scoped?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose (breakdown of the overlay / radial command menu) matches the content.
apparent_themes: contextual navigation from queued comments to anchors
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to define a single, always-available, non-intrusive command surface that lets a mobile user switch modes, manage drafts/submissions, triage replies, and chat with a supervisor without leaving the content view.
novelty: A radial menu tied to a branded 'V' entry point that opens toward the thumb and includes a priority 'needs your eyes' lane for supervisor-facing decisions.
contribution: A structured feature breakdown for a thumb-friendly radial command overlay, capturing the intended control set (mode toggle, draft queue, submit-all, comment navigation, supervisor chat, inbox, settings) and the ergonomic principle that the menu should open toward the thumb.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard design breakdown for a mobile radial 'V' command menu with mode toggles, submission controls, comment navigation, supervisor chat, and a priority inbox lane.
intention_for_embedding: To specify a persistent, thumb-reachable overlay command center that consolidates creation, submission, review, and supervisor communication without blocking content.
